sanskrit: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते । निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥
hindiTranslation: वशमें किया हुआ चित्त जिस कालमें अपने स्वरूपमें ही स्थित हो जाता है और स्वयं सम्पूर्ण पदार्थोंसे निःस्पृह हो जाता है, उस कालमें वह योगी है  ऐसा कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: When the controlled mind rests in the true self alone and is free from craving for all pleasures, that person is called united in yoga.
essence: Yoga begins when craving loses its grip and the mind comes home.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person is truly steady in yoga when the mind settles into the true self and no longer chases every desire.
simpleInsight: There is relief in not needing every craving to be answered.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen, and Krishna is teaching the method of inner mastery. After describing balanced habits, Krishna names the real sign of yoga: a mind that has come home to the true self and no longer runs after desire.
modernRelevance: You finally close the laptop after midnight, but your mind keeps reaching for one more reply, one more check, one more fix. Peace begins when the chasing stops.
translationLiteral: यदा (when) / विनियतं (well-controlled) / चित्तम् (mind) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / एव (alone) / अवतिष्ठते (rests) / निःस्पृहः (free from craving) / सर्वकामेभ्यः (from all desires) / युक्तः (united, disciplined) / इति (thus) / उच्यते (is called) / तदा (then)
insight: It shifts attention from managing impulses to recognizing the mind’s settled state.

quoteVariants.text: The controlled mind rests in the true self. Craving falls away.
The mind is quiet when wanting is gone.
Rest in the true self. Release wanting.
